briefdescription.para: Size of pointer offset in bytes.
Fixed-point accuracy bits for remap calculations.
Descriptor size in bytes.
Address width in bits.
Wait iteration count for flush done.
Timeout for flush done in microseconds.
Macro to align a number up to the nearest multiple of align.
Offset type definition.
This function creates aligned void memory pointer.

parameterdescription.para: is the alignment size.
is the size of pointer to be created.

simplesect.para: ptr is memory pointer to be returned.

briefdescription.para: This function frees the allocated aligned memory.

parameterdescription.para: is the memory pointer to be free.

briefdescription.para: This function does all the ontime calculation for lens distortion which to be done for each input configs change.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to initialization vector.
is the pointer to projective transformation input.

memberdef.argsstring: (XVWarpInit_ArbParam *arbitrary_param, unsigned short fr_width, unsigned short fr_height)
memberdef.name: XVWarpInit_OnetimeCalcsArbt

briefdescription.para: This function does all the ontime calculation for Arbitrary distortion which to be done for each input configs change.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to input Arbitrary parameters.
is the frame width.
is the frame height.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the descriptor into hw.

parameterdescription.para: is pointer to hardware memory(DDR).
is the descriptor data to be set in DDR.

memberdef.argsstring: (XVWarpInit_ArbParam *arbitrary_param, int grid_size, u16 fr_width, u16 fr_height)
memberdef.name: XVWarpInit_AllocArbMem

briefdescription.para: This function allocates the required memory for intermediate arbitrary variables/calculation.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to input Arbitrary parameters.
is the grid size for the arbitrary distortion.
is the frame width.
is the frame height.

briefdescription.para: This function parses the input mesinfo for arbitrary distartion.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to input Arbitrary parameters.
is the pointer to the input mesh information.
is the frame width.
is the frame height.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the mesh information parsing successful XST_FAILURE if the control points are not valid in the given mesh information.

briefdescription.para: This function validates the given configs with IP core configs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon.
is the pointer to input configuration

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the validation is successful XST_FAILURE is any input config is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function Enables the Interrupts for the core instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the Interrupt enable mask

briefdescription.para: This function creates the descriptors.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the number of descriptors to be created

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if Descriptors created successfully XST_FAILURE if Descriptors creation failed

briefdescription.para: This function clears and de-allocates all the descriptors.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon

briefdescription.para: This function programs a descriptor with given configurations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the descriptor number which to be configured
is the input configuration pointer which to be configured into the descriptor

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if programming descriptor is successful XST_FAILURE if input configurations are not valid.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the IP core with a selected descriptor configuration.

detaileddescription.para: /* This function starts the IP core with a selected descriptor configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the descriptor number with which configurations the IP shall start

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if IP core start successful XST_FAILURE if selected descriptor number is not valid.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the core instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon

briefdescription.para: This function converts projective transform coefficients to fixed-point format.

detaileddescription.para: Processes the first two columns (6 coefficients) and last column (3 coefficients) of a 3x3 projective transformation matrix separately with different Q-bit ranges.

parameterdescription.para: is the 3x3 projective transformation matrix (9 elements).
is output fixed-point representation of first 2 columns (6 coefficients).
is output fixed-point representation of last column (3 coefficients).
is output Q-bit position for each of the 6 h coefficients.

memberdef.argsstring: (short *knots_x, short *knots_y, int grid_pts, int len, int *remap_row)
memberdef.name: apply_arbt_warp_line

briefdescription.para: apply_arbt_warp_line - Apply arbitrary warp transformation to a line using cubic interpolation

detaileddescription.para:
knots_x
Array of x-coordinate control points (knots)
knots_y
Array of y-coordinate control points (knots)
grid_pts
Number of grid points/control points
len
Length of the line to process (number of output samples)
remap_row
Output array containing the warped x-coordinates for each pixel
This function applies a cubic spline-based warp transformation to remap pixel positions along a horizontal line. It uses piecewise cubic interpolation with control points (knots) to compute non-linear coordinate transformations.

The algorithm:
Iterates through each output pixel position
For each segment between consecutive knots, computes cubic coefficients (a0, a1, a2, a3)
Uses fixed-point arithmetic with bit-shifting for efficient inverse calculations
Evaluates the cubic polynomial at each point to determine the remapped x-coordinate

simplesect.para: None Results are stored in the remap_row output array.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned short *src_ctrl_pts, int *src_tangents, short fr_size, short grid_size)
memberdef.name: creat_src_tangents

briefdescription.para: This function creates tangent vectors for source control points.

detaileddescription.para: It calculates tangent values used in cubic spline interpolation for arbitrary warp transformations. The function handles two cases: uniform and non-uniform control point spacing.

src_ctrl_pts
Pointer to source control points array to be populated
src_tangents
Pointer to output tangent values array (stores 3 values per segment)
fr_size
Frame size (width or height depending on usage)
grid_size
Number of grid divisions/control point segments
None. Results are stored in src_ctrl_pts and src_tangents arrays Note: Tangent values are encoded as 16-bit value with 8-bit Q-bit position stored in lower 8 bits, and the actual tangent coefficient in upper bits
